story.soThat: logs are easily searchable and parseable for debugging and monitoring
story.tasks: - Task 1: Install Winston dependencies (winston, winston-daily-rotate-file) - Task 2: Create Winston logger configuration (winston.config.ts with JSON format, log levels) - Task 3: Implement console transport for development (colorized, pretty-print, NODE_ENV-based) - Task 4: Implement file transport with rotation (daily rotation, logs/ directory, app-%DATE%.log pattern) - Task 5: Implement environment-based configuration (LOG_LEVEL env variable, defaults based on NODE_ENV) - Task 6: Implement sensitive data scrubbing (password, token, secret, apiKey, creditCard, authorization) - Task 7: Create WinstonLogger service (logger.service.ts, implements NestLoggerService) - Task 8: Integrate logger in main.ts (app.useLogger(), log startup) - Task 9: Write comprehensive tests (unit tests for config, service, scrubbing; integration tests for transports) - Task 10: Documentation and verification (README updates, verify log output in dev/prod modes)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
AC-7.3.1: Winston configured and usable globally via dependency injection - WinstonModule created in src/common/logger/ - Logger service injectable in all modules - Configured in main.ts with app.useLogger()
AC-7.3.2: Log format is JSON structured with fields: timestamp, level, message, context - Consistent schema across all log entries - Timestamp in ISO 8601 format (UTC) - Context object includes: module, method, requestId, userId, domainId
AC-7.3.3: Log levels implemented: debug, info, warn, error - Logger methods: logger.debug(), logger.info(), logger.warn(), logger.error() - Log level filtering based on configuration
AC-7.3.4: Console transport configured for development environment - Colorized output for better readability - Pretty-printed format for development - Only active when NODE_ENV=development
AC-7.3.5: File transport configured for production with daily rotation - winston-daily-rotate-file package used - Log files written to logs/ directory - File naming: app-YYYY-MM-DD.log - Automatic rotation at midnight - Retention: 14 days (configurable via LOG_MAX_FILES) - Max file size: 20MB (configurable via LOG_MAX_SIZE)
AC-7.3.6: Log level is environment-based (dev: debug, prod: info) - LOG_LEVEL environment variable respected - Default: 'debug' in development, 'info' in production - Configurable without code changes
AC-7.3.7: Sensitive data (passwords, tokens) excluded from logs - Automatic scrubbing of sensitive fields - Fields to exclude: password, token, secret, apiKey, creditCard, authorization - Sanitization applied before logging
AC-7.3.8: Log files written to logs/ directory with rotation (app-YYYY-MM-DD.log) - Directory created automatically if doesn't exist - File transport writes asynchronously (non-blocking) - Graceful handling of file write errors

doc.title: Epic 7 Technical Specification - Developer Infrastructure
doc.snippet: Winston configured for structured JSON logging with console (dev) and file (prod) transports. Log format includes timestamp, level, message, context. Sensitive data scrubbing required. Performance target: <5ms overhead per request.

doc.title: Epic 7 Technical Specification - Developer Infrastructure
doc.section: Data Models and Contracts - Winston Log Entry Interface
doc.snippet: LogEntry interface: timestamp (ISO 8601), level (debug|info|warn|error), message (string), context (module, method, requestId, userId, domainId), stack (error traces). JSON structured for machine parsing.

doc.title: Epic 7 Technical Specification - Developer Infrastructure
doc.snippet: WinstonLogger service provides structured logging (JSON format). Inputs: log level, message, context. Outputs: log entry to console/file. Owner: Story 7.3. Global logger set via app.useLogger() in main.ts.

doc.title: Epic 7 Technical Specification - Developer Infrastructure
doc.snippet: winston v3.11.0 (structured logging), winston-daily-rotate-file v4.7.1 (log rotation). Compatible with NestJS v11.x, Node.js >=18.x, TypeScript >=5.0.

doc.title: Epic 7 Technical Specification - Developer Infrastructure
doc.snippet: Passwords, tokens, credit cards MUST be excluded from logs. Implement sensitive field detection (password, token, secret, apiKey, creditCard). Automatic scrubbing in LoggingInterceptor. Request body sanitization before logging.

doc.title: Epic 7 Technical Specification - Developer Infrastructure
doc.snippet: Log entry creation: <1ms overhead per request. File writes: Asynchronous (non-blocking). Winston file transport: Async writes, buffer flushing every 1 second. Target: Logging adds <5ms to total request duration (p95).

doc.title: Epic 7 Technical Specification - Developer Infrastructure
doc.section: Test Strategy Summary - Story 7.3 Unit Tests
doc.snippet: Logger Configuration: Winston logger initializes with correct transports, console (dev), file (prod), log levels respect LOG_LEVEL env. Log Format: JSON structure, timestamp/level/message/context fields, sensitive fields excluded. Coverage target: 85%+.

doc.title: Epic 7: Developer Infrastructure
doc.snippet: Winston configured globally. Log format: JSON structured with timestamp, level, message, context (module, method, domainID, userID). Log levels: debug, info, warn, error. Console transport (development), File transport (production) with daily rotation.

doc.title: Story 7.2: Common Utilities & Decorators
doc.snippet: Pure functions established in src/common/utils/. date.util functions available for timestamp formatting. string.util functions for sanitization patterns. Testing: 100% coverage standard for utilities.

artifact.reason: Main application entry point. Need to add logger initialization with app.useLogger() after creating NestJS app (line 7). Also replace console.log (line 23) with logger.log().
Root module. Need to import LoggerModule in imports array (line 23-54) to make logger globally available across all modules.
Date utility functions from Story 7.2. Can be used for timestamp formatting in logs if needed (already has formatDate function).
String utility with sanitizeInput() function from Story 7.2. Reference pattern for implementing sensitive data scrubbing in logger.
Example of unit test structure from Story 7.2. Follow same Jest patterns (describe, it, expect) for logger tests with 100% coverage target.

npm.reason: Core logging library for structured logging. Provides logger instance, transports, formats.

dependencies.reason: Winston transport for daily log file rotation. Required for AC-7.3.5 (file transport with rotation).
Already installed. Provides LoggerService interface that logger.service.ts must implement.
Already installed. TypeScript types for Node.js (process.env).

constraints.constraint: Winston logger MUST be configured as NestJS LoggerService implementation
Use app.useLogger() in main.ts to set Winston as global logger (all NestJS internal logs use Winston)
Log format MUST be JSON structured in production for machine parsing (ELK, CloudWatch)
Console transport MUST be colorized and pretty-printed in development (NODE_ENV !== 'production')
File transport MUST use winston-daily-rotate-file with pattern app-%DATE%.log in logs/ directory
Log level MUST be environment-based: LOG_LEVEL env var, default 'debug' (dev) or 'info' (prod)
Sensitive fields MUST be scrubbed: password, token, secret, apiKey, creditCard, authorization → '[REDACTED]'
File writes MUST be asynchronous (non-blocking) to avoid request processing delays
Performance target: Log entry creation <1ms, total overhead <5ms per request (p95)
Logger service MUST be @Global() and injectable in all modules without explicit import
Follow existing patterns: src/common/ for shared infrastructure, __tests__/ subdirs for test files
Test coverage MUST be 90%+ for logger configuration, 95%+ for logger service

interface.name: LoggerService
LogEntry
winston.createLogger
DailyRotateFile
interface.signature: new DailyRotateFile({ dirname: string, // Log directory path filename: string, // Filename pattern with %DATE% placeholder datePattern: string, // Date pattern for rotation (YYYY-MM-DD) maxSize: string, // Max file size before rotation (e.g., '20m') maxFiles: string, // Retention period (e.g., '14d') format: winston.Logform.Format })

tests.standards: Jest unit testing framework (already configured). Test files in __tests__/ subdirectories. Unit tests follow Arrange-Act-Assert pattern. Mock external dependencies (file system, winston transports). Coverage targets: 90%+ (config), 95%+ (service). Integration tests verify actual file creation and rotation. Test data: sample log entries with various contexts, sensitive data for scrubbing tests.
tests.ideas: AC-7.3.1: Test logger initializes with correct transports based on NODE_ENV AC-7.3.2: Test JSON log format includes all required fields (timestamp, level, message, context) AC-7.3.3: Test all log level methods (debug, info, warn, error) call correct winston methods AC-7.3.4: Test console transport only active in development (NODE_ENV check) AC-7.3.5: Test file transport configuration (dirname, filename pattern, maxSize, maxFiles) AC-7.3.6: Test LOG_LEVEL env variable overrides default log level AC-7.3.7: Test sensitive fields (password, token, secret) are replaced with '[REDACTED]' AC-7.3.8: Integration test: Verify log files created in logs/ directory with correct filename pattern Performance test: Log entry creation <1ms, 1000 entries <1 second Edge case: Test circular reference in context object logged as [Circular] Edge case: Test log directory not writable → graceful fallback to console only
